summary: The recent arXiv papers focus on advanced sensing, detection, and security for small UAS, highlighting both operational and defensive capabilities. One study explores visual cooperative tracking for open-path gas measurement using Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Spectroscopy, enabling real-time mapping of gas concentrations like CO2 and methane without spatial sampling. Another introduces a multi-physics simulation framework for designing high-power microwave counter-UAS systems, aiming to neutralize autonomous drones without kinetic or RF jamming methods. A third paper presents Diamond, a lightweight authenticated encryption framework tailored for resource-constrained IoT devices such as small drones, ensuring secure telemetry transmission under adversarial wireless conditions. Additionally, YolovN-CBi is introduced as a modified, efficient architecture for real-time detection of small UAVs, addressing challenges posed by their size, speed, and low visual contrast. Finally, SDG-Track proposes a heterogeneous observer-follower framework for high-resolution UAV tracking on embedded platforms, resolving the resolution-speed trade-off by processing native 1080p imagery without losing small target features.
